molecules.name: 7-fluoro-2-(3,4-difluorophenyl)-4-oxochromen-5-yl acetate
molecules.rationale: Top-performing candidate balancing binding affinity with drug-like properties. Incorporates optimized 3,4-difluorophenyl pendant ring with core fluorination to achieve synergistic binding enhancement.

molecules.name: 7-fluoro-2-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-4-oxochromen-5-yl acetate
molecules.rationale: Equally potent isomer to Lead 1, utilizing 2,4-difluorophenyl pattern. Alternative difluoro positional arrangement may offer different metabolic fate and binding orientation compared to 3,4-isomer.

molecules.name: 2-(3,4-difluorophenyl)-4-oxochromen-5-yl acetate
molecules.rationale: High-affinity backup candidate eliminating core fluorination. Simplified synthetic route with improved LogP (0.14 units lower) while maintaining robust binding affinity within 0.1 kcal/mol of Lead 1. Optimal for synthetic accessibility.
key_features: Lower LogP (2.03) for improved drug properties

molecules.name: 7-fluoro-2-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-oxochromen-5-yl acetate
molecules.rationale: Statin-mimetic candidate incorporating 4-fluorophenyl group common to clinical HMGCR inhibitors (Atorvastatin, Rosuvastatin). Conservative design with highest confidence in binding mode validity based on known drug scaffolds.
key_features: Clinical precedent for fluorophenyl HMGCR binding

key_findings: Chromone scaffold superior to naphthalene, anthracene, single rings
Position-5 acetate linker optimal (-8.6 baseline, 0.6-0.8 kcal/mol advantage over other positions)
Fluorination synergy confirmed between 7-position core and difluoro pendant rings
3,4-difluoro and 2,4-difluoro patterns optimize pendant ring (-9.0 kcal/mol)
All leads maintain QED ~0.74, MW 315-333 Da, LogP 2.03-2.17
critical_concerns: Docking scores within ±0.5-1.0 kcal/mol error margin of typical algorithms
Synergy mechanism not mechanistically validated (no binding poses shown)
HMGCR binding mode for position-5 carboxylate not confirmed by crystal structure
All leads cluster at -9.0 to -8.8 kcal/mol (potential scoring saturation)
Single-scaffold strategy creates vulnerability to off-target effects
recommended_next_steps: Dock clinical HMGCR inhibitors as controls for scoring validation
Generate high-resolution binding poses for top 2 leads
Cross-validate with alternative docking software (Glide, PLANTS, GOLD)
Expand SAR with grow_cycle to test 20-30 additional pendant ring variants
Test alternative anionic linkers (carbamate, sulfone, amide variants)
Comprehensive Lipinski evaluation including TPSA and metabolic soft spots

validation_status.drug_properties: PARTIAL (MW, LogP, QED only; missing TPSA, solubility, metabolism)
